summary: Large warning-cleanup pass focused on eliminating implicit function declarations by moving item type/struct definitions out of `it/item.h` into a new `it/types.h`, adding missing prototypes/includes, and propagating already-improved field names such as `Fighter::facing_dir` across fighter code. The PR also began a review-discussed naming cleanup for item callback fields, distinguishing boolean predicates from event callbacks, though the final convention was still being debated in review comments.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/it/types.h` with the large body of item-related constants, flags, structs, callback tables, item attributes, hit/hurt structs, state descriptors, dynamics structs, and the `Item` struct that had previously lived in `it/item.h`.
Shrank `src/melee/it/item.h` substantially by moving item type definitions to `it/types.h`; the review excerpt explicitly says this code was "Moved to `it/types.h`."

Added missing includes and declarations needed to avoid implicit function declaration warnings, for example adding `sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h` and prototypes for `func_80225DD8(HSD_GObj* item, HSD_GObj* owner)` and `func_80225D64(HSD_GObj* item, HSD_GObj* owner)` in `db_unknown_001.h`.
Adjusted type declarations where evidence was clear, such as changing `extern s32 g_debugLevel;` to `extern enum_t g_debugLevel;`.

Reordered includes in low-level string headers such as `src/cstring.h`, moving `<Runtime/platform.h>` before `<stddef.h>`, likely to satisfy project typedef/platform dependencies without implicit or mismatched declarations.

Applied the `Fighter` field rename from `x2C_facing_direction` to `facing_dir` across many fighter files, including Captain, Donkey, Dr. Mario, Fox, CLink, Game & Watch, Luigi, Mario, Marth, Mewtwo, Ness, Pikachu, Samus, Zelda, Master Hand, and shared fighter code.
Touched many fighter special-move files mostly with mechanical call-site updates where facing direction is passed to effects, item spawns, velocity calculations, collision checks, and SFX selection.

Updated item implementation files `item.c` and `item2.c` heavily, with hundreds of line changes, apparently to align with the new item type header and callback/function declarations; the provided excerpt does not expose enough of those hunks to name all affected functions safely.
Added or adjusted declarations in game-mode headers `gmmain_lib.h` and `code_801601C4.h`, and removed one line from `gmmain.c`, consistent with moving prototypes to headers.
Added two lines to `sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.h`, likely missing process-related declarations, but the excerpt does not show the exact symbols.
smart_moves: Separated item type definitions into `it/types.h` rather than continuing to overload `item.h`; this reduces include coupling and makes it easier for other modules to include item structs without pulling in broader item APIs.

Used prototypes with concrete project types such as `HSD_GObj*` instead of leaving callers dependent on implicit `int` return assumptions, which is essential for both compiler correctness and decomp matching on PowerPC ABI call conventions.

Kept a large mechanical rename (`x2C_facing_direction` to `facing_dir`) consistent across call sites rather than mixing old offset names and semantic names, improving readability without changing observed behavior.

Documented item flags and struct fields while moving them, preserving offset-oriented names (`x0_`, `x4_`, etc.) where full semantics were not yet known and adding comments only where evidence existed.
Started callback naming cleanup at the type-definition layer, which is the right place to standardize how item logic table callbacks are read throughout item code.

The review discussion considered how callback names read at call sites, not just in declarations; this is especially useful for decomp code where field names often appear in expressions like `ip->callback(gobj)`.
decomp_lessons: When cleaning implicit function declaration warnings, prefer adding the real header/prototype and required type includes rather than relying on local `extern` declarations or implicit defaults.

Large struct moves are safest when preserving offsets and field order exactly; the new `it/types.h` retained offset-style field names and fixed-size array/comment context, which helps avoid accidental ABI/layout drift.
Header hygiene matters in decomp projects: moving shared structs into a `types.h` can break cycles and avoid including high-level implementation headers just for data layouts.
Semantic field renames such as `facing_dir` should be propagated broadly in one pass to avoid duplicate vocabulary for the same offset.
For callback tables, naming should encode return/behavior only when it improves call-site readability; predicates, event callbacks, and action/update callbacks may need different conventions.
Do not over-interpret unknown item fields: this PR retained many `xNN` names and comments like `unk`, `???`, and offset annotations, which is preferable to inventing semantics during warning cleanup.
Changing an `extern` object type, as with `g_debugLevel` from `s32` to `enum_t`, should be done in the shared header so every translation unit sees the same declaration.
naming_conventions: `Fighter::x2C_facing_direction` was renamed/used as `Fighter::facing_dir` throughout touched fighter code.
Item callback naming discussion: author initially described `if_` as a predicate prefix for callbacks returning `BOOL` and `on_` as an event prefix for callbacks returning `void`.

Review feedback questioned `if_` because call sites could read awkwardly, e.g. conceptually `if (if_jumped_on(...))`; alternatives discussed included `is_`, dropping predicate prefixes entirely, or keeping `on_` only for event callbacks.

Author/reviewer discussion favored `on_` for event-like callbacks such as `on_grab`, `on_anim`, and `on_phys` because without the prefix names like `grab`, `anim`, and `phys` could be mistaken for imperative operations or data fields.
Offset-style names remained acceptable for not-yet-understood fields, e.g. `x0_`, `x4_`, `xB8_itemLogicTable`, and flag bit fields, preserving decomp traceability.

Callback field names in `ItemLogicTable` combine offsets with behavior names, e.g. `x4_callback_OnSpawn`, `x8_callback_OnDestroy`, `xC_callback_OnPickup`, `x18_callback_OnGiveDamage`, and boolean-returning callbacks such as `x24_callback_OnReflect` in the excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The dominant matching tactic was type/prototype correction: adding declarations prevents the compiler from assuming implicit `int` return types and default argument promotions, which can change generated code or warnings.

Mechanical field rename to `facing_dir` preserved expressions and arithmetic structure while improving symbol names; examples include velocity formulas, `atan2f` arguments, SFX array indexing, effect spawns, and collision direction checks.
Include-order adjustments in `cstring.h` suggest a tactic of ensuring project platform typedefs/macros are available before standard declarations are parsed.
Moving structs to `it/types.h` lets implementation files include only the layouts they need, reducing header dependency noise that can otherwise obscure matching differences.
No direct assembly diff or reviewer matching note is present in the provided slice, so claims about exact instruction-level changes beyond prototype/layout hygiene are low-evidence.
review_feedback: Author note on `src/melee/it/types.h`: "Started reworking callback names. `if_` means a predicate (returns a `BOOL`) while `on_` is an event (returns `void`)."
Reviewer feedback objected to the `if_` predicate prefix because call sites could read strangely, suggesting `is_jumped_on` and similar alternatives.
Another review comment questioned whether `is_` might be too suggestive of a boolean field rather than a callback/function.
A review suggestion proposed skipping the predicate prefix entirely so call sites read like `if (jumped_on(...))` or `if (collided(...))`, relying on context to show the result is boolean.
Review discussion separately asked whether to drop `on_` as well.

Author/reviewer discussion defended keeping `on_` because event callbacks like `on_grab`, `on_anim`, and `on_phys` are clearer than bare names such as `grab`, `anim`, and `phys`, which could imply commands like `do_grab`, `update_anim`, or `recompute_phys`.
The excerpt contains 16 review comments total, but only the item header move and callback naming debate are visible in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for the final settled item callback naming convention: `if_`, `is_`, bare predicate names, and `on_`.
Check subsequent changes to `src/melee/it/types.h` for refinements to `ItemLogicTable` callback names and return types.
Search for remaining uses of `x2C_facing_direction` after PR 671 to find files not covered by this pass.
Inspect the full diff of `item.c` and `item2.c` for exact prototype fixes and whether any item functions were renamed or only retyped.
Look for later PRs that split `it/types.h` again if it became too large or introduced include cycles.
Verify whether `g_debugLevel` consistently remains `enum_t` in later headers and source declarations.
Search for remaining implicit-function-declaration warning passes around PRs before/after 671 to reconstruct the full cleanup campaign.
